title: "Desktop resolution really low in 8.04 (please help =( )"

### Post by Solarius on 2008-04-29
I have an NVidia Geforce 6800ultra and a Dell 2005FPW monitor (1600x1050).

Right now, I'm getting some horrible resolution that's totally off.  When I go into the proprietary drivers menu, the Nvidia driver is enabled and status is "in use."

Here's my mess of an xorg.conf, I have a feeling that the problem is in here.

```
# nvidia-xconfig: X configuration file generated by nvidia-xconfig
# nvidia-xconfig:  version 1.0  (buildmeister@builder3)  Thu Feb 14 18:20:37 PST 2008

    InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard" "CoreKeyboard"

EndSection

Section "Module"

    Identifier     "Generic Keyboard"

    Option         "XkbRules" "xorg"

    Option         "XkbLayout" "us"

    ModeLine       "800x600@60" 40.0 800 840 968 1056 600 601 605 628 +hsync +vsync
```

### Post by Zorael on 2008-04-29
You're an upgrader, hmm?

Yes, I'd create a new xorg.conf.
```
$ sudo mv /etc/X11/xorg.conf /etc/X11/xorg.backup0804
$ sudo dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg    # set keyboard layout, etc
$ sudo nvidia-xconfig -d 24 --allow-glx-with-composite --add-argb-glx-visuals --randr-rotation
```

---
